column1: The Progressive strategy initially exhibits behaviour similar to the Aggressive strategy
column2: with low reputation and early ejection due to low honesty. As collateral is depleted
column4: surviving nodes become progressively more honest
column5: allowing reputation to recover toward levels comparable to Conservative nodes. This transition slows further ejections and extends node survival.

column1: The Opportunistic strategy exhibits behaviour similar to the Balanced strategy
column2: as the inverse reputation--honesty relationship causes nodes to converge toward moderate honesty levels. Unlike the Balanced strategy
column4: honesty continuously adapts to reputation. Nodes with declining reputation become more honest
column5: while nodes with high reputation become less honest. This produces greater variation in node behaviour and allows a subset of nodes to recover reputation and remain active for longer periods
column6: resulting in later survival checkpoints than the Balanced strategy.

column2: adaptive behaviour extends node survival relative to their corresponding benchmark strategy (Progressive relative to Aggressive
column3: and Opportunistic relative to Balanced). The longer survival period also increases cumulative exposure to verification and audit penalties
